visualIdentity.summary: Agile female woodland Archer — practical, combat-ready ranger rather than a decorative or ceremonial figure. Reads instantly as a ranged attacker through bow, quiver and a compact, mobile silhouette.
visualIdentity.roleReadability: ranged physical damage dealer — bow and quiver must be legible at reduced Board/Shop scale before any other detail

silhouette.outline: narrow shoulders, compact torso, visible bow arc breaking the outline on one side, short cape/hood breaking the outline behind the shoulders, quiver breaking the outline at the upper back
silhouette.readabilityRule: silhouette must remain identifiable as 'Archer' from outline alone, with no interior color information, at the reduced mobile Board/Shop/Bench scale
silhouette.negativeSpace: keep space between the bow arm and the body open so the bow curve stays separable from the torso at small size

bodyProportion.headToBodyRatio: approximately 1:2 (large head, small body), matching the existing Class 1 chibi cast
bodyProportion.build: slim, agile build — not muscular, not heavyset
bodyProportion.stanceWidth: narrow stance, feet close together, no wide combat straddle

layers.notes: short hem above the knee — legs stay visible and readable, no long robe/skirt that hides the legs
must stay compact — no oversized or floor-length cape; cape may move slightly in idle/move states but must not obscure the bow arm or quiver

layers.piece: warm brown leather chest wrap, forearm guards (bracers), and a simple belt
layers.notes: practical ranger gear, not heavy plate — must not read as Knight/Fighter armor weight

layers.piece: quiver strap crossing the chest/back, small pouch on the belt
layers.notes: strap and quiver silhouette must remain visible even when the cape is layered over the back

costume.footwear: simple laced leather boots, ankle-to-shin height

primary.name: Olive-green hood and cape
primary.usage: hood, cape, largest single color area

accent.name: Muted warm gold trim
accent.usage: quiver strap buckle, cape clasp, bowstring wrap, small stitching details — used sparingly

colorPaletteRationale: Primary olive-green is brighter and more saturated than the Arena Ruins theme's muted moss (#55663F) so the silhouette separates from the floor instead of blending into it; both primary and secondary read clearly against the cool-gray arena masonry (#5D6270). No neon values are used — all hues stay in the hand-painted fantasy range.

hair.style: practical mid-length braid or tied-back ponytail, kept clear of the face and clear of the bow-draw sightline
hair.notes: hair remains visible beneath/around the hood — the hood does not fully enclose the head

faceReadability.shape: feminine face shape, without becoming overly delicate or losing definition at small size
faceReadability.expression: focused, alert gaze toward the front-facing direction
faceReadability.mobileConstraint: eyes and eyebrow line must remain the clearest facial feature at reduced Board/Shop icon scale; avoid fine linework that disappears below ~64px render height
faceReadability.hoodInteraction: hood sits back off the forehead/crown rather than shadowing the eyes, so the face stays readable

weapon.bowDesign: recurve-style fantasy longbow, compact enough to be held beside the body without exceeding the character's own height
weapon.weaponSilhouette: the bow's curve must break the body outline clearly on one side and remain recognizable as a bow — not a staff, not a crossbow — even when the sprite is reduced to Shop-card size
weapon.bowString: visible taut string, no arrow nocked in the idle/reference pose
weapon.quiverPlacement: worn diagonally across the back (strap crossing the torso), quiver mouth visible above the shoulder, arrow fletchings partially visible at rest

pose.referencePose: standing at rest, both feet clearly planted shoulder-width apart, bow held loosely in the lowered/off-hand beside the body, free hand relaxed near the quiver strap
pose.cameraFraming: full body visible head-to-foot with small margin, 3/4 front view matching the Runtime camera convention
pose.footAnchorNote: feet position in this reference pose establishes the visual foot line used for the Runtime anchor (0.5, 0.92); no animation or motion is derived from this task

cameraAndFraming.framing: full body, centered, matching the neutral reference convention already used for Class 1 pilots
cameraAndFraming.consistency: camera angle and framing must match the approved chibi Class 1 reference convention so the Archer sits naturally beside other pilots on the Board

lighting.direction: soft key light from front-upper-left, gentle fill from the front-right
lighting.notes: even, readable lighting suitable for a small mobile sprite — no harsh rim light, no dramatic shadow that could obscure the bow or quiver silhouette; consistent with a neutral hand-painted reference sheet rather than an in-scene lit render

materialGuidance.technique: hand-painted material rendering — soft painterly shading and color blocking, not photorealistic PBR, not flat cel-shaded vector art
materialGuidance.cloth: soft-edged painted shading on cloth and leather, visible but restrained brushwork texture
materialGuidance.metal: small metal accents (buckles, bracer rivets) get a slightly brighter, warmer highlight than cloth, kept minimal
materialGuidance.consistency: material rendering must match the existing approved hand-painted Class 1 chibi style so the Archer does not look like a different rendering pipeline from other heroes

mobileReadabilityConstraints.silhouetteAtSmallSize: outline and bow/quiver readability must survive a reduced-size check equivalent to Board/Bench/Shop-card scale
mobileReadabilityConstraints.colorContrast: primary/secondary colors must remain distinguishable from each other and from the Arena Ruins masonry/moss palette at small size
mobileReadabilityConstraints.detailDensity: avoid fine linework, thin straps, or small pattern detail that disappears or turns to noise below small render sizes
mobileReadabilityConstraints.silhouetteFirst: silhouette and pose must communicate 'Archer' before color or facial detail is legible

transparentBackgroundExportConstraints.background: fully transparent (alpha channel), no baked color fill, no checkerboard placeholder baked into the export
transparentBackgroundExportConstraints.canvasContent: character only — no frame, border, vignette, ground shadow plane, text, numbers, watermark, UI icon, or environment/background scenery baked into the image
transparentBackgroundExportConstraints.edges: clean alpha edges with no premultiplied-alpha halo
transparentBackgroundExportConstraints.cropping: full body must remain inside the canvas with a small safety margin — no cropped hands, bow tips, or cape edge

forbiddenVisualTraits: priest/acolyte silhouette or ceremonial holy-figure presentation
staff or any long ceremonial focus item as the primary weapon
oversized/large sword or heavy blade weapon
long floor-length robe or cloak matching the Mage or Acolyte costume shape
crossbow (must be a hand-drawn bow, not a crossbow)
visual similarity to Ninja, Ranger, or Sniper class silhouettes
oversized cape that hides the legs or obscures the bow/quiver

differentiationFromAcolyte.acolyteReference: Acolyte (Class 1 visual lock) is female, staff or holy focus, light/sacred visual language, combat-ready but ceremonial-adjacent
archerMustDiffer: no staff or holy focus item — Archer's primary silhouette element is the bow
no sacred/ceremonial light-magic visual language — Archer reads as a grounded woodland ranger
no long holy robe silhouette — Archer's costume is short-hemmed and leg-visible
costume material language is leather-and-cloth ranger gear, not cloth-and-holy-trim vestments
differentiationFromAcolyte.rule: silhouette and costume shape must differ clearly from Archer per the Class 1 visual lock contract; this specification enforces the Archer side of that separation

excludedFromThisTask: motion test execution or motion test frames
final artwork or any binary/image asset
canonical approval or canonical file assignment
deferred: user approval of the exact reference image
sprite sheet packing and Runtime import
